calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Sep-22-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Louie for Marcia Beard, who was also present (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on the Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary.

The Court inquired whether there were any objections. No objections were raised. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary. The lost will dated November 27, 2024, is admitted to probate. Marcia Beard is appointed as the Executor with full authority. Bond is set at $772,000, at the petitioner's request. Order for the same signed in open Court. A status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for March 24, 2027, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. If either petition is filed two weeks prior, no appearance is required on that date. Michael Kasolas is appointed probate referee.

Judge: Lydia Villarreal; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Aug-18-2025 Continued To Sep-22-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Louie appeared for Petitioner and Proposed Executor Marcia Beard, who was also present (via Zoom).
George Beard, decedent's brother (via Zoom).

The Court heard the petition for probate of a lost will and for letters testamentary.

George Beard objected to the appointment of Marcia Beard as personal representative and raised concerns regarding her identification in prior filings, the alleged removal and disposition of the decedent's belongings without authority or accounting, uncertainty about the location and disposition of the decedent's remains, and inconsistencies between the petition and his understanding of the decedent's testamentary wishes. He also alleged a lack of communication and transparency among those involved with the estate.

Mr. Louie responded that the objections were premature and should be raised in a written filing. He noted that certain issues would be addressed once a fiduciary was formally appointed. The Court advised Mr. Beard to file written objections and suggested that he seek legal counsel for procedural guidance. The Court further noted that an inventory and appraisal would be required in due course.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to September 22, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow George Beard time to file written objections and for the parties to communicate regarding outstanding concerns.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
